questions.question: The absolute value of $x$ is denoted by $|x|$. If $|x| = 5$, what are the possible values of $x$?
The distance between $-3$ and $4$ on the number line is $|(-3) - 4| = $
Tom has been saving money for a new bike and has $|-25| + 30$ dollars. How much money does Tom have in total?
Samantha's favorite hiking trail is $|8 - 12| + 5$ kilometers long. What is the total length of the trail?
Ashley is $|15 - 20|$ minutes late for her piano lesson. How many minutes late is Ashley?
Ethan wants to buy a new video game that costs $|-30| + 10$ dollars. How much will Ethan pay for the video game?
Jessica has $|12 - 8| cdot 2$ pencils in her backpack. How many pencils does Jessica have?
A bookshelf has $|5 - 9| + 2$ shelves. How many shelves does the bookshelf have?
If $|x + 2| = 7$, what are the possible values of $x$? Show all your work and explain your reasoning.

A bakery is $|10 - 15|$ kilometers away from the city center. If it takes $|5 - 8|$ hours to get to the bakery by car, what is the average speed of the car in kilometers per hour? Show all your work and explain your reasoning.

teacher_rules: Emphasize the importance of understanding absolute value as distance from zero on the number line.
Encourage students to use real-life scenarios to illustrate their understanding of absolute value.
Remind students to check their work and explain their reasoning for free-response questions.
